jokes.text: Wake me when it's over.
jokes.setup: Johnny is woken up on Christmas Eve morning after having an elaborate dream about a successful Christmas performance
jokes.punchline: His immediate response to learning it's Christmas Eve is to ask to be woken when the holiday is over
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character-driven humor. Shows Johnny's complete Christmas avoidance in just five words.

jokes.text: Less shock, deeper depression.
jokes.setup: Johnny explains their first year they were in shock, setting up expectation that last year was better
jokes.punchline: David corrects that it was actually worse - they were less shocked but more depressed
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of expectations with perfect comedic structure and timing. Very quotable.

jokes.text: caroling in front of mom's wig wall?
jokes.setup: Johnny suggests caroling as a festive activity
jokes.punchline: David points out the absurdity of caroling in their current modest living situation with Moira's prominent wig collection
jokes.explanation: Great character-specific humor that calls back to Moira's established eccentricity. 'Wig wall' is inherently funny.

jokes.text: Or the reindeer room.
jokes.setup: Discussion about missing their old Christmas luxuries like grand piano and ice sculptures
jokes.punchline: Moira casually mentions they used to have an entire room dedicated to reindeer
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira absurdity - casually mentioning something completely over-the-top as if it's normal.

jokes.text: unfortunately, Ted and I will be unable to attend
jokes.setup: Johnny asks Alexis to handle the guest list for the party
jokes.punchline: She immediately announces she won't be attending the party she's supposed to be organizing
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Alexis's self-centeredness and formal speaking style, but fairly predictable.

jokes.text: everything on my list requires pre-order
jokes.setup: Discussion about gifts for a last-minute Christmas party
jokes.punchline: Alexis's gift expectations are so high-end they all require advance ordering
jokes.explanation: Good character detail about Alexis's expensive tastes, though the joke is somewhat expected.

jokes.text: somebody needs to find the menorah
jokes.setup: Johnny is enthusiastically planning Christmas celebrations
jokes.punchline: He casually mentions needing to find the menorah, revealing the family's mixed religious traditions
jokes.explanation: Nice casual reveal of the family's religious complexity, delivered naturally within the scene.

jokes.text: I'm a delightful half-half situation
jokes.setup: Patrick assumes David is Jewish when he questions Christmas authority
jokes.punchline: David describes his mixed religious heritage in characteristically precious language
jokes.explanation: Perfect David phrasing - 'delightful half-half situation' is exactly how he would describe mixed heritage.

jokes.text: It's like the 12 Days of Christmas, but it's 1 day with 12 bottles of wine.
jokes.setup: Patrick asks about Stevie's own holiday tradition when she buys two cases of wine
jokes.punchline: She parodies the classic Christmas song to describe her drinking habit
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that perfectly captures Stevie's dry wit and drinking habits. Highly quotable and well-structured.

jokes.text: I'd burned that bridge in Ibiza
jokes.setup: David mentions they can't afford Nate Berkus (celebrity designer) anymore
jokes.punchline: Casually reveals he somehow burned bridges with a famous designer in an exotic location
jokes.explanation: Great character detail suggesting David's dramatic past and tendency to create problems in glamorous settings.

jokes.text: Wow. So, what time is the Ghost of Christmas Past coming to visit you tonight?
jokes.setup: Stevie is being particularly Grinch-like about Christmas decorations and generosity
jokes.punchline: Patrick sarcastically compares her to Scrooge from A Christmas Carol
jokes.explanation: Solid pop culture reference that fits the moment, though somewhat predictable given Stevie's behavior.

jokes.text: Ooh, burn, David!
jokes.setup: Patrick just delivered a sarcastic burn to Stevie
jokes.punchline: Stevie appreciates the insult directed at her, calling it out as a good burn
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Stevie's dry appreciation for wit, even when directed at her.

jokes.text: Nana Bud worked real hard for those.
jokes.setup: David asks about Mardi Gras beads among Christmas decorations
jokes.punchline: Stevie's deadpan comment implies her grandmother earned the beads in the traditional Mardi Gras way (flashing)
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversive humor delivered with perfect deadpan timing. Creates a hilarious mental image.

jokes.text: Do you have time to mood-board a color scheme? - Does anyone? - No.
jokes.setup: David is overwhelmed by party planning demands
jokes.punchline: His rhetorical question about mood-boarding gets literal responses, highlighting the absurdity
jokes.explanation: Good ensemble moment that plays with David's design obsessions and the group dynamic.

jokes.text: Look at you in that flouncy apron, you little Christmas Elf!
jokes.setup: Alexis visits Ted who is baking
jokes.punchline: Her condescending but affectionate observation about his domestic appearance
jokes.explanation: Captures Alexis's slightly patronizing but loving way of talking to Ted.

jokes.text: I'm gonna take that as a compliment, even though everything inside me's telling me that it's not.
jokes.setup: Alexis calls him a 'little Christmas Elf'
jokes.punchline: Ted's awareness that her comment is condescending but chooses optimism anyway
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Ted's self-awareness and positive attitude despite Alexis's backhanded compliments.

jokes.text: But instead of cookies, it was whatever we could find in our parents' medicine cabinets, and instead of a Christmas party, it was an old boot factory in Krakow.
jokes.setup: Ted describes an innocent cookie competition tradition with friends
jokes.punchline: Alexis's 'similar' experience was drug-related in an abandoned factory in Poland
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that perfectly captures Alexis's wild past. The parallel structure makes the contrast even funnier.

jokes.text: Well, I, I did get up at the Krak-ow dawn
jokes.setup: Alexis mentions Krakow in her story
jokes.punchline: Ted makes a pun on 'crack of dawn' using the city name
jokes.explanation: Decent pun that shows Ted trying to keep up with Alexis's stories, though somewhat forced.

jokes.text: they have all agreed to give you one last chance... one... more... final chance.
jokes.setup: Alexis tries to reassure Ted about meeting her friends
jokes.punchline: She accidentally reveals they're giving Ted 'one last chance' then makes it worse trying to correct herself
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Alexis making things worse while trying to fix her mistake.

jokes.text: What if I missed chest and back day?
jokes.setup: Ted asks what if something came up and he couldn't attend the party
jokes.punchline: His hypothetical excuse is missing a specific workout routine
jokes.explanation: Funny character detail showing Ted's commitment to his workout schedule in an unexpected context.

jokes.text: your cool, scary, judgy friends
jokes.setup: Ted talks about meeting Alexis's friends
jokes.punchline: His description reveals he knows exactly what he's getting into with her social circle
jokes.explanation: Good insight into the relationship dynamic and Ted's awareness of Alexis's intimidating friends.

jokes.text: that's just 72 more cookies to make, then.
jokes.setup: Alexis suggests doubling the cookie recipe to bring some to her parents
jokes.punchline: Ted's matter-of-fact calculation of the additional workload
jokes.explanation: Good understated reaction showing Ted's patience with Alexis's casual demands.

jokes.text: And the protestors, shouting at the flatbed truck as it made its way through our front gates.
jokes.setup: Moira recalls their 22-foot Norwegian Pine Christmas tree
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions that their Christmas tree was so excessive it attracted protesters
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira moment - treating environmental protesters as part of the festive atmosphere shows her disconnection from reality.

jokes.text: What you're smelling is actually our holiday-scented car ornaments. They can be used in the car, or on your tree. Sold separately, of course.
jokes.setup: Johnny comments on smelling pine needles at the tree lot
jokes.punchline: Ray reveals it's fake scent from car air fresheners he's trying to sell separately
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Ray's opportunistic sales tactics and the town's low-end everything.

jokes.text: Oh, I'm sorry, by special, I meant higher price, because of demand.
jokes.setup: Ray mentions a special price for the last trees
jokes.punchline: Clarifies that 'special' means more expensive, not discounted
jokes.explanation: Nice misdirection that perfectly captures Ray's business practices and the family's ongoing financial struggles.

jokes.text: 2 gestures for $175.
jokes.setup: Johnny called the tree 'just a gesture'
jokes.punchline: Ray incorporates 'gesture' into his pricing terminology
jokes.explanation: Clever callback that shows Ray's opportunistic nature while playing with language in a funny way.

jokes.text: It depends, are we talking emotionally?
jokes.setup: Johnny asks if he's asked for a lot this year
jokes.punchline: Moira's clarifying question suggests he's been very demanding emotionally
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira response that reveals the marital dynamic while being characteristically blunt.

jokes.text: I hear Peter Fundraiser. Bogdanovich loved a mink.
jokes.setup: Johnny mentions the PETA Christmas Fundraiser where she wore fur
jokes.punchline: Moira mishears 'PETA fundraiser' as 'Peter Fundraiser' and references director Peter Bogdanovich
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira mishearing that leads to an absurd Hollywood reference. Very character-specific humor.

jokes.text: Oh, straight as an arrow.
jokes.setup: Johnny asks if the Christmas tree is straight
jokes.punchline: Moira claims it's perfectly straight when it's obviously crooked
jokes.explanation: Basic visual gag setup, though the humor relies heavily on the visual element not fully captured in transcript.

jokes.text: Perhaps a Christmas gourd.
jokes.setup: David identifies a pumpkin among the Christmas decorations
jokes.punchline: Moira tries to reframe it as something Christmas-appropriate
jokes.explanation: Good Moira attempt to spin something inappropriate into something festive. 'Christmas gourd' is inherently funny.

jokes.text: That was me getting on board.
jokes.setup: Johnny criticizes her Christmas gourd suggestion
jokes.punchline: Moira points out this was her attempt at being supportive as he requested
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Moira's literal interpretation of Johnny's request for support.

jokes.text: That was me getting on board.
jokes.setup: Johnny saying she requested to get on board
jokes.punchline: Moira's dry acknowledgment that Christmas gourd was her effort
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Moira's minimal effort version of cooperation

jokes.text: Nanna Bud worked real hard for those.
jokes.setup: David questions the Mardi Gras beads again
jokes.punchline: Stevie repeats her earlier suggestive comment about how her grandmother earned them
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier joke, though with less impact the second time around.

jokes.text: An... espresso machine. - You're selling coffee now? - Hmm... it's more for the staff room.
jokes.setup: Johnny asks what David is saving money for instead of buying decorations
jokes.punchline: David reveals he's prioritizing an espresso machine for himself over family Christmas decorations
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing David's selfishness and his attempt to make personal luxuries sound business-related.

jokes.text: You're not helping, Moira.
jokes.setup: Moira corrects Johnny about the espresso machine when he's trying to scold David
jokes.punchline: Johnny's exasperated response to Moira's unhelpful precision
jokes.explanation: Good family dynamic moment showing Johnny's frustration with his family's lack of support.

jokes.text: Making it? I thought you wanted me to handle it? Like, work the door, make sure nobody gets in that isn't on the list!
jokes.setup: Johnny is angry that Alexis hasn't made a guest list
jokes.punchline: Alexis reveals she thought 'handling the guest list' meant being a bouncer, not creating it
jokes.explanation: Excellent miscommunication humor that's perfectly in character for Alexis's interpretation of responsibility.

jokes.text: Who's going out crashing other people's Christmas parties?
jokes.setup: Alexis thought she was supposed to work the door as a bouncer
jokes.punchline: Johnny points out the absurdity of party crashers on Christmas Eve
jokes.explanation: Good logical response that highlights how silly Alexis's interpretation was.

jokes.text: You didn't tell him? - Oh, my god. - Okay, I hate this!
jokes.setup: Ted realizes Johnny doesn't know they're not staying for the party
jokes.punchline: The awkward revelation and Alexis's dramatic response to the uncomfortable situation
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing the family communication breakdown and Alexis's dramatic reactions.

jokes.text: everyone low-key hates me. - Uh, hate is a strong word.
jokes.setup: Alexis says Ted's friends all hate her
jokes.punchline: Ted's diplomatic correction doesn't actually deny it, just says 'hate' is strong
jokes.explanation: Good relationship humor showing Ted trying to be diplomatic while not quite denying Alexis's claim.

jokes.text: the Jerry beads
jokes.setup: Discussion about putting Mardi Gras beads on the tree
jokes.punchline: Alexis calls them 'Jerry beads' instead of Mardi Gras beads
jokes.explanation: Good character detail showing Alexis's casual mispronunciation of things she doesn't really understand.

jokes.text: I, for one, find it charming, in sort of a... a war-torn sort of way.
jokes.setup: Everyone is looking at their pathetic Christmas tree setup
jokes.punchline: Moira's attempt at positivity compares their Christmas to a war zone
jokes.explanation: Excellent Moira line that captures her dramatic tendencies and inability to be genuinely reassuring.

jokes.text: Looks like the other side has been charred, or something.
jokes.setup: Trying to rotate the tree to hide its crooked side
jokes.punchline: The other side is even worse - it's been burned
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the tree problem - when they try to fix one issue, they discover a worse one.

jokes.text: Why don't we just cut it up for firewood?
jokes.setup: Various suggestions for salvaging the terrible Christmas tree
jokes.punchline: David suggests abandoning any pretense and just burning it
jokes.explanation: Good David response showing his willingness to give up entirely when things aren't perfect.

jokes.text: Why don't we just cut it up for firewood?
jokes.setup: Alexis suggesting cutting up the tree for garlands
jokes.punchline: David's more practical but defeatist suggestion
jokes.explanation: David's practical cynicism cutting through the pretense

jokes.text: It's meatloaf night at the cafe, and if anything is screaming Christmas to me right now, it's meatloaf.
jokes.setup: Johnny is giving up on the Christmas party in frustration
jokes.punchline: He sarcastically declares meatloaf as the most Christmas-appropriate thing available
jokes.explanation: Good bitter humor showing Johnny's complete defeat and the anti-Christmas nature of their situation.

jokes.text: meatloaf night was yesterday. - Dear God, I...
jokes.setup: Johnny declares he's going for meatloaf at the cafe
jokes.punchline: Twyla reveals meatloaf night was yesterday, making his situation even more pathetic
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that makes Johnny's defeat complete - even his escape plan is wrong.

jokes.text: Literally, Alexis and Stavros are on his father's yacht by now. Off to Capri.
jokes.setup: Johnny asks where the kids are
jokes.punchline: Moira casually mentions Alexis is on a yacht to Capri, as if this is normal
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Alexis's glamorous past life and Moira's casual treatment of luxury as normal.

jokes.text: David, last I heard he was screaming at Wolfgang. Apparently there weren't enough capers on the smoked salmon crudites.
jokes.setup: Johnny asks about David's whereabouts
jokes.punchline: Moira describes David having a tantrum about catering details with celebrity chef Wolfgang Puck
jokes.explanation: Perfect David characterization - having dramatic fits over pretentious food details with famous chefs.

jokes.text: Oh don't worry, I already gave them their checks.
jokes.setup: Johnny wants to open presents with the kids on Christmas
jokes.punchline: Moira reveals she just gave them money instead of actual gifts
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Moira's practical but unsentimal approach to gift-giving.

jokes.text: I've already taken my Christmas pills, and bitter experience has taught me I have just 8 minutes to make it safely up the stairs.
jokes.setup: Johnny asks Moira to stand with him by the tree
jokes.punchline: Moira explains she can't because she's taken medication and has a narrow window to get upstairs safely
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor that reveals Moira's holiday coping mechanisms while being characteristically dramatic.

jokes.text: I could've sworn it was Wednesdays. - It's always been Tuesdays.
jokes.setup: Johnny thanks the kitchen for meatloaf on what he thought was meatloaf night
jokes.punchline: Twyla gently corrects that he's been wrong about the schedule
jokes.explanation: Callback that reinforces Johnny's disconnect from his current life and basic schedules.

jokes.text: And yet, here you are, eating yesterday's meatloaf.
jokes.setup: Johnny claims everything is fine on Christmas Eve
jokes.punchline: Twyla points out he's alone eating day-old meatloaf, contradicting his claim
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation from Twyla that cuts through Johnny's pretense with gentle but pointed truth.

jokes.text: it can, at times, be quite peaceful. - Well, it wasn't too peaceful this afternoon, I'm sorry to say.
jokes.setup: Moira finds the positive in their desolate living situation
jokes.punchline: Johnny points out today was anything but peaceful, referencing the family chaos
jokes.explanation: Good callback that acknowledges the day's events while showing the couple's different perspectives.

jokes.text: It appears there's been a Christmas miracle, John.
jokes.setup: Johnny has given up on Christmas after the disastrous day
jokes.punchline: Moira announces a Christmas miracle as the family returns with decorations
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the emotional turn, using traditional Christmas language to announce the family's return.

jokes.text: We have spent a very long time gluing it back together. Scary amount of super glue. Seriously, nobody should light a match in here.
jokes.setup: Johnny asks if this is the same terrible tree from earlier
jokes.punchline: David reveals they used so much glue to fix it that the room is now a fire hazard
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that shows their resourcefulness while creating a new absurd problem.

jokes.text: Seriously, nobody should light a match in here.
jokes.setup: David mentioning scary amount of super glue
jokes.punchline: Stevie's practical safety concern about fire hazard
jokes.explanation: Classic Stevie - practical concern delivered dryly

jokes.text: You know I hate to miss a good party.
jokes.setup: Alexis shows up after spending the whole episode trying to avoid the party
jokes.punchline: She claims she never misses parties, despite literally trying to skip this one
jokes.explanation: Good ironic callback to her party-loving reputation versus her earlier reluctance.

jokes.text: Ted's vet friends are coming by later, and I have a sneaking suspicion they're a little bit...
jokes.setup: Alexis mentions Ted's vet friends joining the party
jokes.punchline: She trails off with an implied negative judgment about veterinarians
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Alexis's continued snobbery even while being generous.

jokes.text: You still thinking about that espresso machine, aren't ya? - Yes. Yes, I am.
jokes.setup: Johnny notices David looking around the decorated room
jokes.punchline: David admits he's still focused on his personal luxury despite the family moment
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows David's consistency and Johnny's understanding of his son.

jokes.text: I'd appreciate my invitation about a week in advance, okay? - Well, if I have things my way, we'll be making this an annual tradition, so you can save the date now. - No, that's way too far in advance, I can't make that kinda commitment.
jokes.setup: Roland complains about the last-minute invitation
jokes.punchline: When offered a year's notice, he says that's too much advance planning
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy showing Roland's impossible-to-please nature and illogical thinking.

jokes.text: I can't make that kinda commitment.
jokes.setup: Johnny suggesting Roland save the date for next year's Christmas party
jokes.punchline: Roland treating a one-year advance notice as too much commitment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Roland moment - commitment issues about the most basic social planning

jokes.text: Oh, Jocelyn, surely the dentures have been dropped in the glass by now.
jokes.setup: Jocelyn says they were supposed to be caroling at the senior center
jokes.punchline: Moira suggests it's too late because the elderly residents are already in bed
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira dark humor with vivid imagery about elderly people's bedtime routines.

jokes.text: What, it's just nice. Nothing's happening. - Looks like you're crying. - Oh well, I'm not. It's just we're... - We ran out of red!
jokes.setup: Johnny appears to be emotional during the Christmas carol
jokes.punchline: When confronted about crying, he deflects with a weak excuse about wine
jokes.explanation: Good emotional moment that balances sentiment with character-appropriate deflection and humor.

jokes.text: We ran out of red!
jokes.setup: Johnny denying he's crying
jokes.punchline: Moira's practical explanation that they ran out of red wine
jokes.explanation: Nice misdirection - offering alcohol-based explanation for Johnny's emotional state

jokes.text: A bomb? - No, it's an antique tin. - For your wig pins.
jokes.setup: Johnny gives Moira a wrapped gift to open
jokes.punchline: Her first guess is that it's a bomb, then it's revealed as a practical wig accessory
jokes.explanation: Good character moments showing Moira's dramatic assumptions and Johnny's thoughtful but modest gift.

jokes.text: Smaller, smaller, smaller.
jokes.setup: Johnny telling Moira to close her eyes for a surprise
jokes.punchline: Having to repeatedly adjust because Moira's dramatic eye-closing is too theatrical
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy playing on Moira's tendency toward theatrical overperformance

jokes.text: Stickers! - Of old men! - No, they're stamps, from all over the world.
jokes.setup: Alexis opens her gift and misidentifies it
jokes.punchline: She thinks vintage stamps are stickers of old men
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis moment showing her lack of worldly knowledge despite her travels.

jokes.text: It's like being right back in Japan.
jokes.setup: David receives 1,000 Yen as a gift
jokes.punchline: His sarcastic appreciation of the modest foreign currency
jokes.explanation: Good David response showing his ability to find the positive in modest gifts while maintaining his sophisticated persona.

jokes.text: Last thing we want is to have the motel burn down. - Or is it?
jokes.setup: Johnny worries about fire safety with the menorah near the garland
jokes.punchline: Moira's dark suggestion that burning down the motel might not be bad
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor ending that perfectly captures Moira's dramatic worldview and their housing situation.
